title: Say Goodbye to Dry Eyes with 10 Herbal Teas
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dry eyes are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the symptoms of dry eye syndrome by soothing and moisturizing the eyes. They are particularly beneficial for individuals who experience dry, itchy, or irritated eyes due to various factors such as computer usage, dry environments, or certain medical conditions. The best herbal teas for dry eyes include Euphrasia officinalis, Calendula officinalis, Symphytum officinale, Echinacea purpurea, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Aloe barbadensis, Achillea millefolium, Hypericum perforatum, and Althaea officinalis. If left untreated, dry eyes can lead to blurred vision, eye strain, and even long-term damage, significantly impacting daily activities and quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis tea, also known as eye bright, is a herbal remedy that has been used for centuries to soothe dry, itchy, and irritated eyes. The tea is made from the flowers of the Euphrasia plant, which are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes. When consumed as a tea, these beneficial compounds are absorbed into the bloodstream and carried directly to the eyes, where they can help to calm and relax the eye tissues. This can lead to a reduction in dryness, redness, and irritation, making it easier to see and feel comfortable. The anti-inflammatory properties of Euphrasia officinalis tea may also help to alleviate conditions such as conjunctivitis and blepharitis, which can cause dry, itchy eyes.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for dry eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing swelling and irritation that can cause dryness and discomfort.
Tannins are good for dry eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and moisturize the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by dryness.

Rutin is good for dry eyes because it helps to protect and strengthen blood vessels in the eyes, which improves the circulation of oxygen and nutrients to the cornea and helps to prevent damage and inflammation that can cause dryness.
Kaempferol is good for dry eyes because it helps to reduce inflammation in the eyes and increase tear production, which can help to soothe and comfort dry, irritated eyes.

remedies.image_alt: Euphrasia officinalis herbal teas for dry eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Verbascum thapsus herbal tea that helps with dry eyes, start by gathering a handful of dried Verbascum thapsus flowers, also known as mullein flowers. If you don't have dried flowers, you can dry them yourself by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once you have your dried flowers, combine one teaspoon of them with one cup of boiling water in a teapot. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flowers to release their medicinal properties into the water. After the steeping time is up, strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the flowers. Your Verbascum thapsus tea is now ready to drink, and it's recommended to consume it 2-3 times a day to help soothe and moisturize dry eyes. You can also add a bit of honey to taste, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for dry eyes, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with various health issues. When it comes to dry eyes, this plant is useful because it contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants in Calendula officinalis help to reduce oxidative stress, which is a major cause of dry eyes. This is because oxidative stress can damage the delicate tissues in the eyes, leading to inflammation and dryness. By drinking Calendula officinalis tea, you can help to reduce this stress and promote healing in the eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties in the tea also help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, which can contribute to dry eyes. Additionally, the plant's antiseptic properties help to prevent infections that can irritate the eyes and make dry eyes worse. Drinking Calendula officinalis tea regularly may help to soothe and calm dry eyes, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy vision.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for dry eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, allowing them to stay moist and healthy.
Kaempferol is good for dry eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the eyes from damage caused by dryness and irritation.
Tannins are good for dry eyes because they help to reduce inflammation in the eyes and increase tear production, providing relief to people who suffer from dry and itchy eyes.

Flavonoids are good for dry eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the delicate tissues of the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry eye syndrome.

remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal teas for dry eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Verbascum thapsus herbal tea that helps with dry eyes, start by gathering fresh or dried Verbascum thapsus flowers. If you have dried flowers, you can skip the next step. Otherwise, pick a handful of fresh Verbascum thapsus flowers and gently wash them with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, chop the fresh flowers into small pieces and place them in a saucepan. Add 2 cups of water to the saucepan and bring the mixture to a boil. Once boiling, reduce the heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the plant material. Let the tea cool down, then strain it again to remove any remaining sediment. Your Verbascum thapsus herbal tea is now ready to drink. To use it for dry eyes, steep 1 teaspoon of dried Verbascum thapsus flowers (or 1 tablespoon of fresh flowers) in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes, then strain and cool the tea before applying it as a compress to your eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea has been used for centuries to help soothe dry, irritated eyes. This herbal tea is made from the flowers and roots of the Echinacea plant, which is rich in antioxidants and other beneficial compounds. When consumed, these compounds can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, which can contribute to dryness and discomfort. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the eyes and reduce redness. This can be especially helpful for people who spend a lot of time in front of screens, read, or work outdoors, as these activities can cause eye strain and dryness. The antioxidants in Echinacea purpurea tea can also help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and irritation. Drinking Echinacea purpurea tea regularly may help alleviate dry, itchy eyes and promote overall eye health.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for dry eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, promoting healthy tear production and lubrication.

Flavonoids are good for dry eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and protect the delicate tissues in the eyes, allowing them to produce more tears and stay moist.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for dry eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Verbascum thapsus herbal tea for dry eyes, start by gathering dried Verbascum thapsus flowers, also known as Mullein flowers. You can purchase them from a health food store or online. Begin by boiling one cup of water and then add one to two teaspoons of the dried flowers to the water. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the water has reduced slightly and the flowers have lost their color. Strain the liquid into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the flowers. Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking it. It's recommended to drink one to two cups of the tea per day to help relieve dry eyes and soothe irritated eyes. You can also add honey to taste, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tea, also known as comfrey tea, has been used to help with dry eye problems for a long time. This is because it contains special compounds called flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink comfrey tea, these compounds are absorbed into your body and can help to reduce inflammation in your eyes. This is especially helpful for people with dry eyes, as inflammation can make the problem worse. Comfrey tea may also help to soothe and calm the eyes, making them feel more comfortable and less irritated. Some people also use comfrey tea to help heal wounds and promote tissue growth, which can be beneficial for people with damaged or irritated eye tissue. Drinking comfrey tea regularly may also help to promote healthy eye function and improve tear production, which can help to alleviate dry eye symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal teas for dry eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Verbascum thapsus herbal tea that can help with dry eyes, start by gathering dried Verbascum thapsus flowers. You can purchase these online or find them in health food stores. Begin by boiling a cup of water and then removing it from heat. Add a teaspoon of dried Verbascum thapsus flowers to the hot water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their properties. Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids. Let the tea cool down before using it. To use the tea as an eye wash, dip a clean cloth into the cooled tea and gently apply it to your closed eyes, holding it there for a few minutes before rinsing with lukewarm water. You can also drink the tea as is or mix it with other herbal teas to help soothe dry eyes and reduce inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with various health issues. When made into a tea, it can be beneficial for people suffering from dry eyes. The tea contains anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. This can provide relief for people who experience dry, itchy, or irritated eyes. The antioxidants in yarrow tea also help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms. Additionally, the tea's soothing properties can calm the eyes and reduce discomfort. Some people find that drinking yarrow tea helps to improve the quality of their tears, making it easier to keep their eyes moist and comfortable. Drinking yarrow tea can be a natural way to help manage dry eye symptoms and find relief from discomfort.

remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal teas for dry eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Verbascum thapsus herbal tea that helps with dry eyes, start by gathering fresh or dried Verbascum thapsus flowers. If using fresh flowers, gently rinse them in cold water to remove any dirt or debris. Then, place 1-2 teaspoons of the flowers in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. If using dried flowers, simply add 1-2 teaspoons to the cup. Next, pour 8 oz of boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to release their active compounds. After the steeping time, carefully remove the tea infuser or cup from the heat and let it cool slightly. Strain the tea into another cup to remove the flowers, and then discard the solids. Your Verbascum thapsus tea is now ready to drink. To use it for dry eyes, simply dip a clean cloth into the tea, let it cool, and gently apply it to your closed eyes for a few minutes to help soothe and moisturize them.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is a natural remedy that can help soothe dry eyes. The tea is made from the gel of the aloe vera plant, which is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. When you drink aloe vera tea, the nutrients are absorbed into your body and can help to keep your eyes moist and comfortable. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, making them feel better. The tea can also help to reduce the risk of eye infections and promote healthy eye tissue. Drinking aloe vera tea regularly may help to alleviate dry eye symptoms and improve vision. The tea is easy to make at home by steeping aloe vera gel in hot water, making it a convenient and natural way to soothe dry, irritated eyes.

If using fresh herbs, gently dry them by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once dry, you can use them to make tea. To make the tea, combine one teaspoon of dried mullein flowers or leaves with one cup of boiling water in a saucepan. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove any solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste if needed. After drinking the tea, you can also apply a cooled tea bag to your closed eyes for a few minutes to help soothe and moisturize dry eyes. This herbal tea is a natural way to relieve dry, itchy, and irritated eyes, and it's especially helpful when consumed regularly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tea is a great remedy for dry eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. The gel from the aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to soothe and calm the eyes. When you drink aloe vera tea, these compounds are absorbed into your body and then released into your eyes, helping to reduce dryness and discomfort. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Verbascum thapsus herbal tea that helps with dry eyes, start by gathering a few dried Verbascum thapsus flowers, also known as mullein flowers. You will need about a teaspoon of the dried flowers for every cup of water you plan to use. Next, boil water in a pot and then remove it from the heat source. Add the dried Verbascum thapsus flowers to the hot water and let them steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the water. After the steeping time has passed, strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot, making sure to discard the solids. You can then drink the Verbascum thapsus tea hot or iced, sweetened with honey if desired. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of the tea a day to help soothe and moisturize dry eyes, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a natural herb used to make tea. This tea is good for dry eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the eyes. When you have dry eyes, the eyes get irritated and inflamed, which can be very uncomfortable. The antioxidants in Melissa officinalis tea help reduce inflammation and promote healing. Additionally, the tea has a relaxing effect on the body, which can help reduce stress and eye strain. This can be especially helpful if you spend a lot of time in front of a computer or read for long periods, as these activities can cause eye strain and dryness. remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal teas for dry eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Verbascum thapsus herbal tea for dry eyes, start by gathering some dried or fresh plant material, such as the flowers, leaves, or roots of the mullein plant. If using fresh material, dry it in a low-temperature oven (150-200°F) for 1-2 hours to preserve its properties. Next, measure out 1-2 teaspoons of the dried plant material for every 8 oz of boiling water. Pour boiling water over the plant material in a teapot or a cup, and let it steep for 5-10 minutes. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the plant material, and discard the solids. Your tea is now ready to drink. To use it for dry eyes, simply dip a clean cloth in the tea, wring it out, and apply it to your closed eyes for 5-10 minutes. Alternatively, you can drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and moisturize your eyes from the inside out.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis tea, also known as marshmallow root tea, is a natural remedy for dry eyes. This tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the eyes. The root of the marshmallow plant contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that coats and shields the eyes from dryness and irritation. When we drink Althaea officinalis tea, the mucilage is absorbed into the body and travels to the eyes, where it creates a protective barrier that locks in moisture and prevents dryness. This helps to reduce redness, itchiness, and irritation associated with dry eyes. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and promote healing in the eyes, making it an effective remedy for people who suffer from dry eye syndrome. Drinking Althaea officinalis tea regularly can help to keep the eyes hydrated and healthy.

remedies.image_alt: Althaea officinalis herbal teas for dry eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Verbascum thapsus herbal tea that helps with dry eyes, start by gathering fresh Verbascum thapsus flowers, also known as mullein flowers. Choose the flowers in full bloom as they contain the highest amount of mucilage, a natural gel-like substance that soothes and protects the eyes. Rinse the flowers gently with water to remove any dirt or debris, then chop them finely to release their oils and mucilage. Next, combine 1 tablespoon of chopped Verbascum thapsus flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has cooled down to a comfortable temperature. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids. Drink the Verbascum thapsus tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate dry eyes and promote eye comfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus tea, also known as mullein tea, is a natural remedy that can help with dry eyes. The leaves of the mullein plant contain saponins, which are special compounds that can help to reduce inflammation in the eyes. When we have dry eyes, it's often because the tear ducts in our eyes are not working properly, and this can cause irritation and discomfort. Drinking mullein tea can help to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing the redness and itchiness often associated with dry eyes. The saponins in the tea can also help to clean out the tear ducts, promoting the flow of tears and helping to keep the eyes moist. This can make a big difference in people who suffer from dry eyes, helping to reduce the discomfort and make their eyes feel more comfortable.

remedies.image_alt: Verbascum thapsus herbal teas for dry eyes
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Verbascum thapsus herbal tea to help with dry eyes, start by gathering a handful of dried Verbascum thapsus flowers, also known as mullein flowers. Place the flowers in a clean glass teapot or infuser and add 250ml of boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flowers to release their medicinal properties into the water. Once the mixture has cooled slightly, strain the tea into a cup using the infuser or by pouring it through a fine-mesh sieve lined with a coffee filter. Discard the used flowers and add a spoonful of honey to taste, if desired, to help soothe the eyes. Finally, let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature and use it as a wash to help alleviate dry, irritated eyes, or drink it as a warm tea to promote overall eye health and relaxation.

supplementary_best: The best combination for dry eyes is a blend of Euphrasia officinalis, Calendula officinalis, and Verbascum thapsus.  Euphrasia officinalis helps to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing redness and inflammation. It also protects the cornea, the clear layer on the front of the eye, from damage.  Calendula officinalis has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease irritation in the eyes. It also promotes healing and protects the delicate tissues around the eyes.  Verbascum thapsus, also known as mullein, is a natural demulcent that forms a protective barrier over the eyes, locking in moisture and soothing dryness. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce redness and irritation.  This combination is effective because it addresses the three main issues associated with dry eyes: inflammation, irritation, and dryness. By combining the soothing and protective properties of these three herbs, you can provide relief from dry, itchy, and irritated eyes.
supplementary_avoid: If you have dry eyes, there are some herbal teas you should avoid.  One of these is tea made from the leaves of the Black tea plant (Camellia sinensis). This is because it can cause dehydration, which will make dry eyes worse. Black tea also contains caffeine, which can reduce tear production.  Another tea to avoid is one made from the roots of the Dandelion plant (Taraxacum officinale). While dandelion is often used to treat eye problems, its diuretic properties can cause more fluid to leave the body, worsening dry eyes.  You should also avoid tea made from the flowers of the Chamomile plant (Matricaria chamomilla). Although chamomile is often used to calm the eyes, its ability to reduce inflammation can sometimes cause the eyes to produce less tears.  Lastly, tea made from the bark of the Willow plant (Salix alba) should be avoided. Willow bark contains salicylic acid, which can reduce inflammation but also cause the eyes to produce fewer tears.
supplementary_other_remedies: Omega-3 fatty acids found in fish oil can help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to dryness. This is because omega-3s have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the eyes and promote healthy tear production. Another natural remedy is Aloe vera gel, which has moisturizing properties that can help hydrate the eyes and surrounding skin. When applied as eye drops or directly to the eyelids, Aloe vera gel can help lock in moisture and reduce dryness. The gel also has antibacterial properties that can help prevent infections. Additionally, applying a warm compress to the eyes can help loosen up any blockages in the tear ducts and promote tear production. This is because the warmth can increase blood flow to the area, which can help stimulate the tear glands.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with dry eyes often experience other eye problems, such as blurred vision, eye irritation, and eye fatigue. This is because the tears that help keep the eyes moist also protect them from dust and other tiny particles. When the eyes are dry, it's harder for them to see clearly, and they can become easily irritated.  Some medicinal herbs that may help alleviate these symptoms include:  Aloe vera, which has soothing properties that can calm the eyes and help them produce more tears.  Eyebright, a herb that reduces swelling and helps to keep the eyes moist.  Ginkgo biloba, which improves blood flow to the eyes and can help them produce more tears.  Marshmallow root, a natural anti-inflammatory that can soothe the eyes and help them feel more comfortable.  Slippery elm, a herb that can help to protect the eyes from irritation and keep them moist.  These herbs may help alleviate dry eye symptoms by promoting the production of tears, reducing inflammation, and soothing the eyes.
